title: Supplementary code and data: Key factors for quantitative precipitation nowcasting using ground weather radar data based on deep learning
author: Daehyeon Han
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This repository contains the source code, examples of pre-trained models, and the dataset from the paper titled "Key Factors for Quantitative Precipitation Nowcasting Using Ground Weather Radar Data Based on Deep Learning." The names of the pre-trained models can be interpreted as follows: 

 Example: ConvLSTM_korea_2km_12_12_3_mmh_crop_filter_seed0_mse.pth 

 
	 Model: ConvLSTM 
	 Data: Radar data from South Korea 
	 12_12_3: 12 input steps (at 10-minute intervals), 12 output steps, and 3 steps of stride to avoid excessive data overlap. 
	 mmh: Trained in mm/h units. 
	 filter: Skips batches with no precipitation. 
	 seed0: The random seeds for NumPy and PyTorch were set to 0. 
	 mse (or bmse): Loss function is (balanced) MSE. 
 

 For more information, you can visit the GitHub repository or contact me directly. 

  GitHub Repository

	 Model: ConvLSTM 
	 Data: Radar data from South Korea 
	 12_12_3: 12 input steps (at 10-minute intervals), 12 output steps, and 3 steps of stride to avoid excessive data overlap. 
	 mmh: Trained in mm/h units. 
	 filter: Skips batches with no precipitation. 
	 seed0: The random seeds for NumPy and PyTorch were set to 0. 
	 mse (or bmse): Loss function is (balanced) MSE.
